data.title: Benzene prices journey southward in Asia
data.description: Benzene prices trended downward in Asia on Wednesday.An industry source in Asia informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices were assessed lower on the back of a bearish purchase pulse in the Asian markets."On Wednesday, FOB Korea benzene prices were assessed at the USD 720-730/mt levels, a drop of USD (-15/mt) from Tuesday's assessed levels.CFR China benzene prices on Wednesday were assessed at the USD 730-740/mt levels, a day on day decline of USD (-20/mt).Meanwhile, downstream SM prices on Wednesday were assessed at the USD 905-915/mt FOB Korea levels, down USD (-15/mt) from Tuesday.

data.title: Workspace Group Rental Income, Pricing Rise
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Workspace Group said both rent owed to it and pricing rose in its fourth quarter, though it expects recent macroeconomic events and slower economic growth to continue to hit customer sentiment.

The office-rental company warned Thursday that the impact of larger tenants leaving, a trend it expects to continue in the first half of fiscal 2026, will take time to overcome but said it remains focused on things it can control. This includes costs as it continues to prioritize a recovery in occupancy and rent roll, an industry metric reflecting rental revenue owed by tenants.

Workspace said that for the period ended March 31, rent roll increased by 0.8% to 139.4 million pounds ($184.6 million). On a like-for-like basis, rent roll grew 1.4% in the quarter to 107.9 million pounds.

Lettings activity was strong, with 390 new lettings completed and total rental value rising by 15% on quarter to 10.1 million pounds, a 15% rise from the fourth quarter of the year before.

Like-for-like rent per square foot grew 0.9% on quarter to 48.08 pounds, while like-for-like occupancy fell by 1 percentage point to 85.1% in the period.

Looking ahead, Workspace said it expects customer demand to be quieter in the first quarter due to the timing of bank holidays.

London-listed shares were down 0.2% in early trade at 425.50 pence.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: UK's Dunelm rises to 3-month high on robust results
data.description:
** Shares of British homeware retailer Dunelm up 6.23% at 1,011p, highest since January 2025

** Top gainer on London's mid-cap index

** Co sees biggest intraday gain since July 2024

** DNLM posts 6.3% YoY growth in Q3 FY25 sales to 462 million pounds ($611.60 million)

** Co expects to meet FY25 PBT guidance of between 204 million pounds to 214 million pounds

** Peel Hunt analysts say, "Management is alive to any opportunities that may open up due to tariff disruption, notably any window to secure lower factory gate pricing or other advantage"

** Up to Wednesday's close, stock fell 7.06% so far this year

($1 = 0.7554 pounds)

data.title: REG-Correction: AS Tallinna Vesi will hold an Investor Conference Webinar to introduce the results for the 1st quarter of 2025
data.description:
AS Tallinna Vesi invites its shareholders, investors, analysts, and other stakeholders to join its investor conference webinar, introducing the results for the 1st quarter and 3 months of 2025.

The webinar is scheduled for 25 April 2025 at 10:00 am (EET) and will be held in English. The webinar will be hosted by Aleksandr Timofejev, Chief Executive Officer, and Taavi Gröön, Chief Financial Officer.

The questions will be answered by Aleksandr Timofejev and Taavi Gröön after the presentation. We encourage participants to send their questions before the webinar takes place, by 9:00 am (EET) on 25 April at the latest, to kommunikatsioon@tvesi.ee. Questions can also be asked during the webinar.

To join the webinar, please register via following link.

The registration will be open until 25 April at 9:00 am (EET). You will receive a link to the webinar.

The webinar will be recorded and made available online for everyone on the company’s website at www.tallinnavesi.ee and on AS Tallinna Vesi YouTube account.

Taavi Gröön

Chief Financial Officer

AS Tallinna Vesi

(372) 62 62 200

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Q1 Earnings, Revenue Rise; Issues Q2 Revenue Outlook
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing reported Q1 earnings Thursday of 69.72 New Taiwan dollars ($2.12) per diluted American depositary receipt, up from NT$43.48 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected NT$67.32.

Net sales for the quarter ended March 31 were NT$839.25 billion, up from NT$592.64 billion a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected NT$827.59 billion.

The company said it expects Q2 revenue to be in a range of $28.4 billion to $29.2 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $26.92 billion.

data.title: LG Electronics Expands Advanced Materials Business with New Glass Powder
data.description:
LG Electronics is expanding its advanced materials business with the launch of LG PuroTec, an antimicrobial glass powder, the electronics products maker said Thursday.

LG PuroTech is designed to inhibit bacteria and fungi in products ranging from appliances and textiles to medical devices, the release said.

With a strong foothold in Asia, including China, Vietnam, and India, LG Electronics plans to expand PuroTec into Europe and North America, it said.

Shares of LG Electronics rose nearly 1% at market close.

data.title: Alpha Group Sets Sights on US and Singapore in Client-Led Expansion Strategy
data.description:
Alpha Group International (LSE: ALPH), the London-based financial services provider, is exploring expansion into Singapore and the United States as part of its long-term growth strategy, according to the company's annual report published today (Thursday)

Alpha Group Eyes Expansion into Singapore and US Markets

The FTSE 250 firm, which reported a 23% increase in revenue to £135.6 million for fiscal year 2024, indicated that the potential expansion would primarily focus on serving existing European clients who already operate in these jurisdictions.

"We see a potential long-term opportunity to extend our offering, in a measured way, beyond Europe, unlocking new markets and revenue streams in the US and Singapore," the company stated in its annual report. "Many of our existing European clients already operate in these jurisdictions and have expressed an appetite for us to service their needs in North America and South East Asia."

The expansion plans appear to be client-driven rather than a rapid geographic expansion initiative. Alpha Group emphasized that the move would create "an exciting opportunity to quickly increase wallet share with firms that already know and trust us."

Private Markets Drive Alpha's Push

The company's Private Markets division, which serves investment managers across private equity, venture capital, private debt, infrastructure, real estate and fund of funds, would likely spearhead this international growth. The division reported a 20% revenue increase to £69.0 million in 2024.

Clive Kahn, who succeeded founder Morgan Tillbrook as CEO on January 1, 2025, highlighted the company's growth potential in his first statement as chief executive.Clive Kahn, Source: Alpha Group

"I am excited by the number of product and market opportunities, the majority of which remain in relatively nascent stages," Kahn said. "I am confident in the scale of untapped demand for our products and services and our ability to execute effectively and deliver sustainable growth for all stakeholders."

The potential expansion aligns with Alpha's strategy of providing banking alternatives and financial risk management services to corporate and private market clients. The company currently operates across 11 international offices, primarily in Europe, with additional locations in Canada and Australia.

£218 Million in Cash

Alpha's business model includes currency and interest rate risk management, accounts and payments services, fund finance, and multi-bank connectivity solutions through its Cobase subsidiary, acquired in December 2023.Dame Jayne-Anne Gadhia

Dame Jayne-Anne Gadhia, who became Chair in November 2024, noted in the annual report that the company's strong cash position of £218 million provides "flexibility for continued investment" to maximize growth potential.

Any expansion would likely build upon Alpha's existing technology platform and expertise before establishing significant physical infrastructure in new markets. The company has not announced specific timelines for the potential moves into Singapore and the US.

Alpha Group completed its transition from London's AIM market to the Main Market in 2024 and subsequently joined the FTSE 250 index in June of that year.

data.title: Siemens Energy Shares Jump After 2025 Outlook Lift
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

Siemens Energy shares jumped after the German group raised its outlook for the year, a move that analysts say signals no material impact from U.S. tariffs.

Shares rose 12% to 64.60 euros in early morning trade in Europe on Thursday.

The company said late Wednesday that it now expects revenue to grow between 13% and 15% in the year ending September, up from a previous 8%-10% range.

Its profit margin before special items should be 4% to 6%, up from a previous forecast of between 3% and 5%.

Net income should be up to 1 billion euros ($1.14 billion), excluding assumed positive special items following the demerger of the energy business from Siemens Limited, India, it added.

Pretax free cash flow was also updated to around 4 billion euros.

For its second quarter, the company posted 14.43 billion euros in orders, up 52% on year, while revenue increased 21% to 9.96 billion euros.

J.P. Morgan analysts said Siemens Energy's second-quarter results were very strong and therefore the timing of its guidance upgrade isn't surprising, despite the uncertainty created by U.S. tariffs.

"The magnitude of guidance upgrade and implied H2 margins show that the company isn't expecting any material impact from U.S. tariffs," they wrote in a note to investors.

However, the analysts cautioned that it might still be a bit early for Siemens Energy to experience weakening demand due to the U.S. tariffs.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: Semiconductor Firm Alphawave IP Won't Issue Guidance Due to U.S. Tariffs
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Alphawave IP Group said it was unable to issue any guidance for the current year due to uncertainty related to U.S. tariffs.

"Due to current global economic uncertainty and the rapidly developing nature of the recently imposed tariff regimes, we aren't in a position to provide guidance for full year 2025 or beyond at this point in the financial year," the U.K. semiconductor company said Thursday.

For the 12 months to Dec. 31, the company's pretax loss narrowed to $32.9 million from $39.5 million a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization declined 18% to $51.1 million, but was above the company's guidance of around $50 million. The corresponding margin was down to 17% from 19%.

Revenue fell to $307.6 million from $321.7 million, while bookings increased 34% to $515.5 million.

Earlier this month, the San Diego-based company Qualcomm said it was considering making an offer to acquire the company. At that time, Alphawave advised its shareholders to take no action as there is no certainty that a firm offer will be made.

In early European trading, Alphawave shares were down 2.7% at 117.50 pence. Shares have risen 39% in the year to date.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: QXO Prices Public Offering at $13.25 per Share
data.description:
QXO said late Wednesday it priced its public offering of about 37.7 million shares at $13.25 per share.

The company said the offering, expected to close on April 21, would finance a portion of the consideration for the pending acquisition of Beacon Roofing Supply.

It added the offering is not contingent on the consummation of the acquisition.

QXO said it granted the underwriters of the offering an option to purchase up to an additional 5.6 million shares at the public offering price minus discounts and commissions.

data.title: India's equity benchmarks shrug off early jitters; jump more than 1% led by HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank
data.description:
** India's Nifty 50 and Sensex rebound from early losses to rise more than 1% amid sustained buying in financials

** HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank jump 1.7% and 2.6%, respectively, to hit record highs ahead of earnings over the weekend

** Financials rise 1.7% on the day and 7.5% in four sessions on strong growth prospects

** Wipro down 4.6% after missing Q4 revenue expectation and forecasting sales decline in ongoing quarter

** Information technology index down 0.7%, sole loser among major sectors

** Broader mid- and small-caps up around 0.5% each

** Waaree Renewable Technologies up 9% on surge in Q4 earnings

FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

Morgan Stanley & Co. International plc

(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:

     Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree

Direct Line Insurance Group plc

(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:

Direct Line Insurance Group plc

(d) Date dealing undertaken:

16 April 2025

     If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"

data.title: Greece's Public Power Corp CEO to stay on until 2028
data.description:
Georgios Stassis will keep his role as CEO of Greece's biggest power utility Public Power Corp (PPC) for a third three-year term, the company said on Thursday.

A PPC shareholders meeting on Wednesday approved Stassis' reelection as CEO until April 15, 2028, PPC said in a bourse filing.

Since taking over in 2019, Stassis has helped to overhaul state-controlled PPC and expand its activities to Romania. The company last week announced a 5.75 billion euro ($6.54 billion)plan to set up a green energy and technology hub in northern Greece.

($1 = 0.8798 euros)
